prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a black background with very dim centered text. The text appears to read “A Different Kind Of Test Drive,” though it is faint and low-contrast.

By the final sampled frame, the screen is still black, now showing a centered Oldsmobile brand end card. A small red Oldsmobile emblem appears above the word “OLDSMOBILE” in large white capital letters. Beneath it is a thin horizontal line and the tagline “THE POWER OF INTELLIGENT ENGINEERING” in smaller white capital letters.

No people, vehicles, settings, or actions are visible in the sampled frames beyond these title/brand graphics.
